---
title: "A year after Meta tapped Alexandr Wang to build a new AI model, Zuckerberg has to sell it - CNBC"
date: 2026-06-14 12:00:02 +0000
category: news
subcategory: other

description: "Meta's CEO Mark Zuckerberg is reportedly under pressure to sell an AI model developed by Alexandr Wang after a year of development."
---

Meta is facing significant pressure as CEO Mark Zuckerberg must consider selling an AI model developed by Alexandr Wang, a year after engaging him for the project. This situation arises amid growing scrutiny of Meta's AI ambitions and the competitive landscape that continues to evolve rapidly.

The AI model, which was expected to enhance Meta's capabilities in the artificial intelligence space, has not yet met the anticipated benchmarks for success. Reports indicate that internal assessments may have raised concerns about the model's performance and its alignment with Meta's strategic objectives. The urgency of the situation is underscored by the competitive pressures from other tech giants, such as Google and Microsoft, which are aggressively advancing their own AI initiatives. These companies have made substantial investments in AI, leading to a race for innovation that Meta cannot afford to lag behind in.

Wang, who is known for his work with Scale AI, was brought on board to spearhead this project with high expectations. However, as the year progressed, it became evident that the model's development faced challenges that could hinder its deployment. The need for a viable product has intensified, especially as Meta seeks to regain its footing in the tech industry following a series of setbacks and criticisms regarding its focus and direction. As noted by [CNBC](https://news.google.?oc=5&hl=en-US&gl=US&ceid=US%3Aen), the pressure to sell the model reflects a broader trend in the industry where companies must demonstrate tangible results to justify investments in AI.

For users and investors, this situation raises questions about Meta's long-term strategy in AI and its ability to compete effectively. If the model is sold or shelved, it could signal a shift in Meta's approach to AI development, potentially leading to a reevaluation of its priorities and resources. The competitive context is critical, as other players in the market continue to innovate and capture user interest, making it essential for Meta to act decisively.

Looking ahead, the next steps for Meta will be crucial, as the company must navigate the complexities of AI development while responding to market demands and internal expectations.
